accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and verified context: EO 14323 was issued July 30, 2025, declaring a national emergency to impose duties on Brazilian imports, terminated under NEA section 202.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the summary tracks the bill and adds no false context.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill's purpose as terminating the Brazil-related national emergency.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly cites the National Emergencies Act section 202 (50 U.S.C. 1622) as the operative authority for termination.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill is a simple one-sentence termination with no exceptions or carve-outs.

effective_dates.why: The bill specifies no effective dates or deadlines, and the summary correctly notes the absence of delayed dates.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the bill's sole operative effect: terminating the declared national emergency.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and free of editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized and internally consistent.

concise.why: The summary is brief and free of padding.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names Executive Order 14323, the National Emergencies Act section 202, and 50 U.S.C. 1622.

correct_figures.why: The stated date (July 30, 2025) and citation match the bill text and verified record.
